subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused, and revisited throughout the essay, demonstrating sharp conceptual alignment.
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and consistent thematic cohesion.
Methodology outlines purposeful source selection with ethical considerations and caseâstudy design.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks a full systematic record of search strategy (e.g., keyword strings, database log) and a defended rationale for date limits.
Contextual background on pandemic restrictions is not explicitly tied back to adolescent anorexia nerviosa.
Conclusion omits discussion of practical applications or methodological implications for future research.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the methodology falls short of full systematic transparency, the student presents a clear, bilingual title, a sharply focused research question, and consistent thematic through-line. The methodology demonstrates informed selection and ethical rigour, satisfying the top-band descriptors for communication, research question formulation and method. Therefore, a full 6 marks is justified.

strengths: Accurate and consistent use of psychological terminology (e.g., IMC, comorbilidad, anÃ¡lisis transversal) throughout the report.
Effective integration of prevalence statistics and clinical definitions to substantiate key points.
Clear understanding of evidence-synthesis methodology demonstrated in outlining inclusion/exclusion criteria.
weaknesses: Occasional imprecision in clinical definitions (e.g., conflating pica with broader eating disorders).
Factual inaccuracies in BMI range notation and lack of quantitative effect sizes or confidence intervals.
Table 2 lacks clear variable labeling, reducing reader comprehension.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good subject knowledge and applies sources effectively, but occasional conceptual inaccuracies and a limited pool of studies prevent an excellent rating. The use of terminology is consistently accurate, meriting top-band performance for that strand, so 5 of 6 marks appropriately reflect strong but not flawless knowledge and understanding.

strengths: Excellent selection of recent, peer-reviewed studies directly relevant to the research question, showing strong research quality.
Analysis is clear and mostly well-supported, with appropriate statistical interpretation (P-values, comparative tables).
Well-structured, critical evaluation of methodological strengths and limitations, including ecological validity and generalizability.
weaknesses: Justification for choice of statistical tests is not tied to data distribution assumptions.
Limited critique of cultural context in transferability and sensitivity analyses for confounders.
Future research directions are only briefly noted, missing deeper thematic synthesis grouping core factors.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation displays good breadth and depth of research, clear and supported analysis, and a coherent, well-structured argument with critical evaluation. Minor gaps (e.g., test rationale, cultural critique) stop short of the very highest band, placing the work solidly in the 7-9 range. Allocating 9 of 12 marks reflects strong critical thinking with room for deeper evaluation.

strengths: Consistent adherence to IB structural conventions with a clear title page, contents, headings, and conclusion.
Layout and formatting are applied correctly: double-spacing, numbered pages, labeled tables and figures, consistent citation style.
Word count and required formatting elements appear correctly, enhancing readability and professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all top-band descriptors. Structure is fully appropriate and layout considerations are correctly applied throughout. There are no significant formatting issues, justifying the maximum 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Psychological terminology (IMC, comorbilidad, anÃ¡lisis transversal/longitudinal, mortalidad experimental) is used **accurately and consistently** in Spanish. Definitions are provided where needed, and technical language is applied correctly when critiquing each studyâs design.

Because precision is maintained across the essay with only very minor lapses, the strand meets the top-band standard.

Text is double-spaced, margins are uniform, tables and figures are labelled, and citations follow a consistent format. Word count, session and page numbers appear correctly.

Minor issues (occasional long URLs affecting spacing) are negligible, so layout and formatting meet the highest rubric expectations.

The student demonstrates solid grasp of DSM-5 criteria, prevalence statistics and methodological terms (validez ecolÃ³gica, muestreo intencional). Sources are generally integrated to substantiate claims, and critical comments on sample representativeness show understanding.

Nevertheless, occasional factual imprecision (e.g., BMI range formatting, conflation of symptom vs. disorder) and reliance on a limited pool of studies restrict the depth of subject knowledge, positioning the work in the **good but not excellent** 3-4 band.

The methodology is outlined (chronological literature review, inclusion criteria, ethical considerations) and draws on five peer-reviewed studies of varying design, indicating purposeful selection. However, the process remains partially implicit: no systematic keyword string, database log, or full inclusion/exclusion matrix is supplied, and the rationale for limiting to 2020-2022 is not defended.

These gaps show a **mostly complete** but not fully rigorous method, aligning with the 3-4 band.

The student presents a concise, bilingual title and immediately states the investigationâs purpose and adolescent focus. Throughout the essay, the writing returns consistently to the effect of COVID-19 on anorexia nerviosa, signalling a clear through-line despite brief references to broader eating-disorder categories. Section headings, topic sentences and transitions all reinforce the central theme, so the reader is never in doubt about the studyâs scope.

Minor slippageâsuch as the opening definition of âtrastornos alimentariosâ before narrowing to anorexiaâdoes not hamper overall clarity. Consequently, the topic is communicated **accurately and effectively**, matching the top-band descriptor.

All five core sources are recent, peer-reviewed, and directly address COVID-19 and adolescent anorexia, demonstrating **good breadth and strong relevance**. While the search is not exhaustive, the selection remains focused on answering the research question, and non-academic references are confined to background statistics.

The research question is expressed precisely in Spanish, operationalised (âen quÃ© medidaâ¦â) and revisited in the introduction, body and conclusion. Each study is explicitly linked back to this question, demonstrating strong conceptual alignment between question and discussion.

Because the question confines the investigation to adolescents and the COVID-19 period, its focus is both **sharp and manageable**, fully satisfying the highest-band criteria.

All prescribed IB structural elementsâtitle page, abstract substitute, contents, numbered sections, conclusion and referencesâare present and logically ordered. Headings mirror the table of contents, supporting reader navigation.

This adherence to convention satisfies the top band for structure appropriateness.

The discussion progresses logically from individual study critiques to an integrated conclusion about isolation and social media as mediating factors. The student evaluates methodological strengths (triangulation, statistical tests) and weaknesses (generalizability, absence of causality) and considers alternative explanations.

Although future research directions are only briefly noted, the argument is **well-structured with critical evaluation**, meriting a high 7-9 band score.

The student compares study designs, sample sizes and statistical outcomes, drawing evidence-based links to the research question. Limitations (e.g., cultural specificity, lack of causal inference) are acknowledged, and numerical findings are interpreted correctly.

Analysis is **clear and mostly well-supported**, though occasional opportunities for theory-driven synthesis are missed, fitting the upper range (7-9) rather than the highest 10-12 band.

annotations.comment: Table 1 is well labeled with clear column headings and consistent units, facilitating quick comparison of clinical case characteristics.
BMI categories are rendered in mixed HTML/math tags rather than uniform LaTeX ($$â¦$$), reducing consistency; format all mathematical expressions with LaTeX for clarity.
The choice of statistical tests is described but lacks justification based on data distribution characteristics; add rationale to bolster methodological transparency.
The methodology section clearly outlines caseâstudy design, intentional sampling criteria, and ethical considerations, demonstrating informed selection of sources.
Although religious fasting is noted as a confounder, the study does not propose methods to disentangle pandemic effects from cultural practices; consider sensitivity analyses.
The discussion notes ethical approval but lacks critical assessment of how cultural contexts limit transferability; a deeper critique would enhance analysis.
Table 2 lacks descriptive labels for the row on exercise and eating behaviors; clarify variable names to improve reader comprehension.
The evaluation correctly identifies ecologicalâvalidity limitations, demonstrating analytical depth regarding sample representativeness and generalizability.

The title is clearly stated at the reportâs start, accurately reflecting the focus on COVID-19 and adolescent eating disorders, fulfilling the requirement to present the investigationâs title prominently.
The research question is precisely operationalized in Spanish and revisited throughout the introduction, providing a sharply focused foundation for the investigation.
The final conclusion succinctly highlights social isolation and social media as mediating factors, providing a coherent synthesis aligned with the research question.
The table of contents is wellâstructured with clear hierarchies and accurate page references, supporting effective navigation and adherence to IB conventions.
Some subsection numbering in the table of contents (e.g., repeating âII . Iâ) is inconsistently formatted; standardize numbering style to improve visual consistency.

The definition of âtrastornos alimentariosâ here conflates pica with broader eatingâdisorder categories, indicating a conceptual misunderstanding; refine clinical definitions to align with DSM-5.
Integration of pre- and post-pandemic prevalence statistics from PÃ©rez (2021) and Bustamante (2023) demonstrates effective source application to substantiate the introduction.
The introduction opens broadly on eating disorders before narrowing to anorexia nerviosa; focusing the narrative on anorexia earlier would strengthen thematic cohesion.
Presentation of P-values and year-by-year comparisons in Table 3 supports well-supported conclusions and demonstrates analytical rigor.
Clear section headings for âIV. Estudio 2022â and its subsection enhance structural coherence and support intuitive navigation.
Multiple statistical methods (t-test, chi-square, Mann-Whitney U) are applied appropriately, demonstrating rigorous analysis of clinical and frequency data.
The conclusion overstates the role of isolation without triangulating patient-reported data; integrating qualitative perspectives would strengthen the argument.
The transition into the conclusion section is structurally clear, reinforcing the reportâs logical progression and reader orientation.
The BMI severity ranges are ambiguously written (e.g., â16-19,9 kg/mÂ²â); revise to precise notation such as $$16.0\leq IMC<19.9\,kg/m^2$$ for greater accuracy.
The description of DSM-5 anorexia severity levels (mild, moderate, severe, extreme) is accurate and demonstrates strong command of clinical terminology.
The overview of pandemic restrictions provides context but is not directly linked to adolescent anorexia nerviosa; explicitly tie these measures back to the research focus.
The ethical evaluation and dense case descriptions strengthen credibility by demonstrating careful methodological appraisal and adherence to research ethics.
The tableâs border widths and cell alignment vary across columns, disrupting visual consistency; standardize formatting to enhance readability.
Several long URLs in the references disrupt spacing; using DOIs or shortened URLs would improve formatting and readability.
DOI formatting is inconsistent across reference entries; ensure uniform presentation of DOIs and URLs to maintain professional consistency.
Consistent page numbering is present throughout the document, satisfying the formatting requirement for pagination.
The rationale for excluding non-English and small-sample studies is stated but lacks justification; provide clearer criteria to mitigate selection bias.
Statistical notation (e.g., â>90%â) is presented without consistent formatting or LaTeX; adopt a uniform notation standard to improve clarity.
The systematic search process (PubMed search and inclusion/exclusion criteria) is clearly outlined, reflecting strong understanding of evidenceâsynthesis methodology.
The integration of findings on food insecurity, social isolation, and social media demonstrates effective thematic synthesis across multiple study designs.
While limitations on causal inference are noted, discussion could be strengthened by referencing causal frameworks or models to deepen the evaluation.
The conclusion outlines key findings but omits discussion of how these insights could inform future methodology or practical applications; consider adding this perspective.
The summary omits specific effect sizes or confidence intervals; including quantitative metrics would strengthen the depth of source application.
Employing a four-year retrospective comparative design directly addresses the research question and enhances research quality and relevance.
The conclusion reiterates study summaries extensively without synthesizing thematic insights; grouping findings by core factors would enhance critical evaluation.
The references section is comprehensive and follows consistent citation formatting, enhancing the reportâs scholarly presentation.
